notes.note: A tribunus plebis designatus had been elected to office but had not yet entered it; in 43 BC tribunes-elect held a politically volatile position between the outgoing year's alliances and the power struggles ahead.

Cicero quotes the Greek word emphatikoteron (transliterated in the Latin as e)mfatikw/teron) — the Greek rhetorical term for a more vivid or forceful expression; the interplay of Greek and Latin registers was characteristic of Cicero's intimate correspondence.

notes.anchor: He has been advanced by a kindness of Antony's
notes.note: Antony, as consul in 44 BC, had wide powers of patronage and appointment; Clodius owed his tribunate to Antony's backing, which made his loyalty to the republican cause potentially suspect in Brutus's eyes.

notes.anchor: the two cannot both come safely through
notes.note: Cicero is saying that Antony and the republican cause (personified by Brutus and Cicero) cannot coexist; L. Clodius has chosen the republican side despite owing his position to Antony.
